title: Authors and Citation
text: Stamm (2026). fiber: S7 Data Structures Diffusion MRI Tractography. R package version 0.2.0, https://github.com/tractoverse/fiber.
code: @Manual{,   title = {fiber: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography},   author = {Aymeric Stamm},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/tractoverse/fiber}, }

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
text: fiber package provides three S7 classes — streamline, bundle, bundle_set — representing diffusion MRI tractography data R, together concise set methods:

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
text: streamline stores: @points — numeric matrix size P×3P \times 3 (number points three spatial dimensions). columns must named "X", "Y", "Z". @point_data — named list numeric-vectors length PP (number points). Stores additional per-point attributes (e.g. fractional anisotropy). @streamline_data — named list per-streamline scalars (length-1 values, type, e.g. mean FA character label). bundle stores: @streamlines — list streamline objects. @streamline_data — named list per-streamline vectors length SS (number streamlines). Attributes common streamlines lifted automatically construction time pushed back individual streamlines subsetting. @bundle_data — named list bundle-level scalars (length-1 values, type). bundle_set stores: @bundles — list bundle objects (optionally named, e.g. subject session IDs "sub-01", case names transferred bundle attribute @bundle_data called id_from_input_list). @bundle_data — named list per-bundle vectors length BB (number bundles). Attributes common bundles lifted automatically construction time pushed back individual bundles subsetting. @set_data — named list set-level scalars (length-1 values, type).

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
text: can install development version fiber GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("tractoverse/fiber")

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
code: library(fiber)  # Build a helix streamline (50 points) t <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 50) sl <- streamline(   points = cbind(X = cos(t), Y = sin(t), Z = t / (2 * pi)) ) sl #>  #> ── Object of class `fiber::streamline()` with 50 points. ── #>  #> • Point attributes: none #> • Streamline attributes: none #>   # Shape descriptors get_curvilinear_length(sl) #> [1] 6.358015 get_sinuosity(sl) #> [1] 6.358015 head(get_curvature(sl)) #> [1] 1.537338e-05 1.625127e-02 4.592627e-02 8.636675e-02 1.352354e-01 #> [6] 1.906608e-01  # Bundle two streamlines sl2 <- streamline(   points = cbind(X = cos(t) * 1.1, Y = sin(t) * 1.1, Z = t / (2 * pi)) ) b <- bundle(list(sl, sl2)) b #>  #> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle()` with 2 streamliness and [50–50] points per streamline. ── #>  #> • Point attributes: none #> • Streamline attributes: none #> • Bundle attributes: none #>   # Reparametrize to 20 points each b20 <- reparametrize(b, n_points = 20L) b20 #>  #> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle()` with 2 streamliness and [20–20] points per streamline. ── #>  #> • Point attributes: none #> • Streamline attributes: none #> • Bundle attributes: none #>   # Hausdorff distance compute_hausdorff_distance(sl, sl2) #> [1] 0.1  # Multi-subject: collect bundles from two subjects into a bundle_set b_sub01 <- bundle(   streamlines = list(sl),   bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-01") ) b_sub02 <- bundle(   streamlines = list(sl2),   bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-02") ) bs <- bundle_set(list("sub-01" = b_sub01, "sub-02" = b_sub02)) bs #>  #> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle_set()` with 2 bundles and [1–1] streamlines per bundle. ── #>  #> • Point attributes: none #> • Streamline attributes: none #> • Bundle attributes: "subject" and "id_from_input_names" #> • Set attributes: none #>  bs[["sub-01"]] #>  #> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle()` with 1 streamlines and [50–50] points per streamline. ── #>  #> • Point attributes: none #> • Streamline attributes: none #> • Bundle attributes: "subject" and "id_from_input_names" #>

what: add_shape_descriptors() method for bundle objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-bundle-method
title: add_shape_descriptors() method for bundle objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-bundle-method
text: Adds shape descriptors bundle. Scalar descriptors (euclidean_length, curvilinear_length, sinuosity) stored length-S vectors bundle@streamline_data. Per-point descriptors (curvature, torsion) stored individual streamline's @point_data. accessible via subsetting push-(bundle[[]]).

title: add_shape_descriptors() method for bundle objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-bundle-method
text: x bundle object. descriptors character vector shape descriptors add. Defaults available descriptors: c("euclidean_length", "curvilinear_length", "sinuosity", "curvature", "torsion").

title: add_shape_descriptors() method for bundle objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-bundle-method
text: bundle specified shape descriptors added.

title: add_shape_descriptors() method for streamline objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-streamline-method
text: Adds multiple shape descriptors single streamline object.

title: add_shape_descriptors() method for streamline objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-streamline-method
text: x streamline object. descriptors character vector shape descriptors add. Defaults available descriptors: c("euclidean_length", "curvilinear_length", "sinuosity", "curvature", "torsion").

title: add_shape_descriptors() method for streamline objects — add_shape_descriptors-fiber-streamline-method
text: streamline specified shape descriptors added @streamline_data @point_data slots appropriate.

what: Adds shape descriptors to a streamline or bundle — add_shape_descriptors
title: Adds shape descriptors to a streamline or bundle — add_shape_descriptors
text: add_shape_descriptors() S7 generic computes number shape descriptors streamline object stores @streamline_data @point_data slots appropriate, methods available following classes: fiber::bundle fiber::streamline function provides convenient way compute shape descriptors attach streamline bundle objects. See documentation individual shape descriptor function (e.g. get_euclidean_length(), get_curvilinear_length(), get_sinuosity(), get_curvature(), get_torsion()) details descriptor computed. bundle objects, scalar descriptors (euclidean_length, curvilinear_length, sinuosity) stored length-S vectors bundle@streamline_data. Per-point descriptors (curvature, torsion) continue stored individual streamline's @point_data. accessible via bundle[[]]@streamline_data bundle[[]]@point_data respectively, subsetting push-mechanism.

title: Adds shape descriptors to a streamline or bundle — add_shape_descriptors
code: add_shape_descriptors(   x,   descriptors = c("euclidean_length", "curvilinear_length", "sinuosity", "curvature",     "torsion") )

title: Adds shape descriptors to a streamline or bundle — add_shape_descriptors
text: x streamline bundle object. descriptors character vector shape descriptors add. Defaults available descriptors: c("euclidean_length", "curvilinear_length", "sinuosity", "curvature", "torsion").

title: Adds shape descriptors to a streamline or bundle — add_shape_descriptors
text: object class x specified shape descriptors added appropriate slots.

title: Adds shape descriptors to a streamline or bundle — add_shape_descriptors
code: # add multiple shape descriptors to a single streamline sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) sl <- add_shape_descriptors(   sl,   descriptors = c("euclidean_length", "curvilinear_length", "sinuosity") ) sl@streamline_data$euclidean_length #> [1] 0.8047799  # add multiple shape descriptors to a bundle sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(20), Y = runif(20), Z = runif(20))) b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl, sl2)) b <- add_shape_descriptors(   b,   descriptors = c("euclidean_length", "curvilinear_length", "sinuosity") ) b@streamline_data$euclidean_length  # length-2 vector #> [1] 0.8047799 0.5586424

text: bundle object.

title: Coerce an object to a bundle — as_bundle
text: Currently supported input classes: streamline: wrapped single-element bundle (lossless). bundle: returned unchanged. dwiFiber (dti): fiber becomes streamline. per-point direction vectors (columns 4–6 @fibers) stored @point_data$direction_x, @point_data$direction_y, @point_data$direction_z. Tracking metadata (method, minfa, maxangle) stored scalars @bundle_data. Vector metadata (ddim, ddim0, voxelext, orientation) stored individual scalar entries (e.g. voxelext_1, voxelext_2, voxelext_3).

title: Coerce an object to a bundle — as_bundle
code: sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) b  <- as_bundle(sl) b@n_streamlines  # 1 #> [1] 1

title: Coerce an object to a bundle_set — as_bundle_set

title: Coerce an object to a bundle_set — as_bundle_set
code: sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl)) bs <- as_bundle_set(b, name = "sub-01") bs@n_bundles  # 1 #> [1] 1

text: x streamline bundle object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Coerce a streamline or bundle to a dwiFiber object — as_dwifiber
text: S4 object class dwiFiber (dti).

title: Coerce a streamline or bundle to a dwiFiber object — as_dwifiber
text: Per-point direction vectors taken @point_data$direction_x, @point_data$direction_y, @point_data$direction_z present; otherwise estimated via finite differences coordinates (forward difference first point, backward difference last, central differences ), unit-normalised. Bundle-level metadata stored @bundle_data keys method, minfa, maxangle, level, source transferred corresponding dwiFiber slots present. Vector-valued fields ddim, ddim0, voxelext, orientation must stored individual scalars (e.g. ddim_1, ddim_2, ddim_3) reconstructed vectors dwiFiber object.

title: Coerce a streamline or bundle to a dwiFiber object — as_dwifiber
code: if (requireNamespace("dti", quietly = TRUE)) {   sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5)))   b  <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl))   dfi <- as_dwifiber(b)   class(dfi)  # "dwiFiber" } #> Warning: RGL: unable to open X11 display #> Warning: 'rgl.init' failed, will use the null device. #> See '?rgl.useNULL' for ways to avoid this warning. #> [1] "dwiFiber" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "dti"

code: sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) identical(as_streamline(sl), sl)  # TRUE — identity coercion #> [1] TRUE

what: Combine bundles and/or bundle_sets into a single bundle_set — bind_bundle_sets
title: Combine bundles and/or bundle_sets into a single bundle_set — bind_bundle_sets
text: Accepts mix bundle objects bundle_set objects. bundles collected flat list wrapped new bundle_set. Bare bundle arguments may optionally named; unnamed bundles included without name label.

title: Combine bundles and/or bundle_sets into a single bundle_set — bind_bundle_sets
code: bind_bundle_sets(..., bundle_data = NULL, set_data = NULL)

title: Combine bundles and/or bundle_sets into a single bundle_set — bind_bundle_sets
text: ... bundle objects bundle_set objects combine. Named bare bundle arguments carry name resulting set. bundle_data named list per-bundle vectors attach resulting bundle_set. Defaults @bundle_data first bundle_set input present. set_data named list set-level scalar metadata attach resulting bundle_set. Defaults set_data first bundle_set input (present) empty list.

title: Combine bundles and/or bundle_sets into a single bundle_set — bind_bundle_sets
text: bundle_set containing input bundles.

title: Combine bundles and/or bundle_sets into a single bundle_set — bind_bundle_sets
code: sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) b1 <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl)) b2 <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl))  # two named bare bundles bs <- bind_bundle_sets("sub-01" = b1, "sub-02" = b2) bs@n_bundles   # 2 #> [1] 2 bs@bundle_data$if_from_input_list  # c("sub-01", "sub-02") #> NULL  # combine two bundle_sets bs1 <- bundle_set(list("sub-01" = b1)) bs2 <- bundle_set(list("sub-02" = b2)) bs_all <- bind_bundle_sets(bs1, bs2) bs_all@n_bundles  # 2 #> [1] 2

what: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
text: Accepts mix streamline bundle objects. streamlines collected flat list wrapped new bundle. bundle_data first bundle argument () preserved; pass via bundle_data argument override. Similarly streamline_data.

title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
code: bind_bundles(..., streamline_data = NULL, bundle_data = NULL)

title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
text: ... One streamline bundle objects. streamline_data named list per-streamline vectors attach resulting bundle. Defaults @streamline_data first bundle input one present. bundle_data named list bundle-level scalar metadata attach resulting bundle. Defaults empty list (bundle_data first bundle input one present bundle_data supplied).

title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
text: bundle containing input streamlines.

title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
code: sl1 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) b1 <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl1)) b2 <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl2))  # combine two bundles b_all <- bind_bundles(b1, b2) b_all@n_streamlines  # 2 #> [1] 2  # mix a bundle and a loose streamline b_mixed <- bind_bundles(b1, sl2) b_mixed@n_streamlines  # 2 #> [1] 2

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
text: bundle ordered collection streamline objects representing tractogram white-matter bundle. stores three compartments: @streamlines — list streamline objects. @streamline_data — named list per-streamline vectors (type), length \(S\) (number streamlines). Values common streamlines may lifted automatically individual streamlines' @streamline_data slots construction time. @bundle_data — named list scalars (length-1 values, type) holding bundle-level metadata.

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
code: bundle(streamlines = list(), streamline_data = list(), bundle_data = list())

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
text: streamlines list streamline objects. streamline_data named list per-streamline vectors length S. supplied, @streamline_data keys common streamlines lifted automatically. bundle_data named list bundle-level scalar metadata.

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
text: bundle S7 object.

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
text: following methods defined bundle objects: format(x, ...): Returns cli-formatted string describing bundle object. print(x, ...): Prints formatted string console invisibly returns x. length(x): Returns number streamlines (equivalent x@n_streamlines). x[[]]: Extracts -th streamline bundle, bundle-level @streamline_data pushed back streamline. x[]: Returns new bundle containing selected streamlines, @bundle_data subset @streamline_data preserved.

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
text: @n_streamlines integer scalar giving number streamlines bundle (read-). @streamline_attributes character vector names per-streamline attributes stored bundle level (read-). @bundle_attributes character vector names bundle-level attributes (read-).

title: Bundle S7 class — bundle
code: sl1 <- streamline(   points = cbind(X = 0:4, Y = 0:4, Z = 0:4),   streamline_data = list(mean_FA = 0.6, label = "CST") ) sl2 <- streamline(   points = cbind(X = 1:3, Y = 1:3, Z = 1:3),   streamline_data = list(mean_FA = 0.7, label = "CST") ) # mean_FA and label are common to both streamlines and are lifted b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl1, sl2)) b@n_streamlines  # 2 #> [1] 2 b@streamline_attributes  # c("mean_FA", "label") #> [1] "mean_FA" "label"   b@streamline_data$mean_FA  # c(0.6, 0.7) #> [1] 0.6 0.7  # Subsetting pushes streamline_data back down b[[1]]@streamline_data$mean_FA  # 0.6 #> [1] 0.6 b[1]@streamline_data$mean_FA    # c(0.6) #> [1] 0.6

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
text: bundle_set collection bundle objects, designed multi-subject multi-session studies element represents one subject's (session's) tractogram. stores three compartments: @bundles — list bundle objects (names optional). @bundle_data — named list per-bundle vectors (type), length \(B\) (number bundles). Values common bundles may lifted automatically individual bundles' @bundle_data slots construction time. @set_data — named list scalars (length-1 values, type) holding set-level metadata.

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
code: bundle_set(bundles = list(), bundle_data = list(), set_data = list())

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
text: bundles list bundle objects (may named unnamed). bundle_data named list per-bundle vectors length B. supplied, @bundle_data keys common bundles lifted automatically. set_data named list set-level scalar metadata.

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
text: bundle_set S7 object.

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
text: following methods defined bundle_set objects: format(x, ...): Returns styled character string. print(x, ...): Prints formatted string console invisibly returns x. length(x): Returns number bundles. x[[]]: Extracts -th (named) bundle set, set-level @bundle_data pushed back bundle. x[]: Returns new bundle_set containing selected bundles, @set_data subset @bundle_data preserved.

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
text: @n_bundles integer scalar giving number bundles set (read-). @bundle_attributes character vector names per-bundle attributes stored set level (read-). @set_attributes character vector names set-level attributes (read-).

title: Bundle set S7 class — bundle_set
code: sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = 1:5, Y = 1:5, Z = 1:5)) b1 <- bundle(   streamlines = list(sl),   bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-01") ) b2 <- bundle(   streamlines = list(sl),   bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-02") ) # subject is common across bundles and lifted to bundle_set@bundle_data bs <- bundle_set(bundles = list("sub-01" = b1, "sub-02" = b2)) bs@n_bundles          # 2 #> [1] 2 bs@bundle_attributes  # "subject" #> [1] "subject"             "id_from_input_names" bs@bundle_data$subject  # c("sub-01", "sub-02") #> [1] "sub-01" "sub-02"

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() catch-all method — compute_hausdorff_distance,any-method
text: compute_hausdorff_distance() catch-method

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() catch-all method — compute_hausdorff_distance,any-method
text: return value; always throws error unsupported input types.

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for bundle objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-bundle-method
text: Dispatches one three behaviours depending y:

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for bundle objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-bundle-method
text: x bundle object. y NULL, streamline, bundle.

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for bundle objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-bundle-method
text: dist object size x@n_streamlines y NULL bundle. lower triangle stores pairwise symmetric Hausdorff distances (computed C++). Use .matrix() obtain full \(n \times n\) matrix. numeric vector length x@n_streamlines y streamline.

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for bundle objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-bundle-method
text: y = NULL: pairwise distances within x dist object size \(n\), computed C++ via single linear loop. y streamline: numeric vector distances y streamline x. y bundle: dist object concatenation streamlines x y.

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for bundle objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-bundle-method
code: sl1 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl1, sl2))  # pairwise dist object (size 2) compute_hausdorff_distance(b) #>           1 #> 2 0.4681201 as.matrix(compute_hausdorff_distance(b)) #>           1         2 #> 1 0.0000000 0.4681201 #> 2 0.4681201 0.0000000  # distances from sl1 to each streamline in b compute_hausdorff_distance(b, sl1) #> [1] 0.0000000 0.4681201

what: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for two streamline objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-streamline-method
title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for two streamline objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-streamline-method
text: compute_hausdorff_distance() method two streamline objects

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for two streamline objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-streamline-method
text: x streamline object. y streamline object.

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for two streamline objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-streamline-method
text: non-negative numeric scalar equal \(\max(d_H(x \y),\, d_H(y \x))\), \(d_H(\B) = \max_{\} \min_{b \B} \|- b\|_2\) directed Hausdorff distance. core computation performed C++ via hausdorff_distance_cpp().

title: compute_hausdorff_distance() method for two streamline objects — compute_hausdorff_distance-fiber-streamline-method
code: sl1 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) compute_hausdorff_distance(sl1, sl2) #> [1] 0.7950254

what: Computes the Hausdorff distance between streamlines — compute_hausdorff_distance
title: Computes the Hausdorff distance between streamlines — compute_hausdorff_distance
text: compute_hausdorff_distance() S7 generic computes symmetric Hausdorff distance streamline objects based 3-D coordinate matrices, methods available following classes: fiber::bundle fiber::streamline four dispatch cases : streamline + streamline: returns single numeric scalar — symmetric Hausdorff distance two streamlines. bundle + missing: returns symmetric numeric distance matrix dimension \(n \times n\), \(n\) number streamlines bundle, giving pairwise Hausdorff distances. bundle + streamline: returns numeric vector length \(n\) giving Hausdorff distance y streamline x. bundle + bundle: returns symmetric numeric distance matrix dimension \((n_x + n_y) \times (n_x + n_y)\), treating concatenation streamlines x y one collection.

title: Computes the Hausdorff distance between streamlines — compute_hausdorff_distance
code: compute_hausdorff_distance(x, y = NULL)

title: Computes the Hausdorff distance between streamlines — compute_hausdorff_distance
text: x streamline bundle object. y streamline bundle object, NULL (default). NULL x bundle, pairwise distance matrix within x returned.

title: Computes the Hausdorff distance between streamlines — compute_hausdorff_distance
text: non-negative numeric scalar x y streamlines. dist object size \(n\) x bundle y NULL bundle (use .matrix() expand full \(n \times n\) matrix). numeric vector length \(n\) x bundle y streamline.

title: Computes the Hausdorff distance between streamlines — compute_hausdorff_distance
code: sl1 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10)))  # streamline x streamline -> scalar compute_hausdorff_distance(sl1, sl2) #> [1] 0.566281  # bundle x missing -> pairwise dist object b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl1, sl2)) compute_hausdorff_distance(b) #>          1 #> 2 0.566281 as.matrix(compute_hausdorff_distance(b)) #>          1        2 #> 1 0.000000 0.566281 #> 2 0.566281 0.000000  # bundle x streamline -> vector compute_hausdorff_distance(b, sl1) #> [1] 0.000000 0.566281  # bundle x bundle -> combined pairwise matrix b2 <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl2)) compute_hausdorff_distance(b, b2) #>          1        2 #> 2 0.566281          #> 3 0.566281 0.000000

what: fiber: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography — fiber-package
title: fiber: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography — fiber-package
text: Provides three S7 classes — streamline, bundle, bundle_set — representing diffusion MRI tractography data R, together concise set methods computing shape descriptors (arc-length, curvature, torsion, sinuosity), Hausdorff distance streamlines, arc-length reparametrization streamlines bundles onto uniform grids, combination streamlines bundles single bundle, combination bundles multiple subjects sessions bundle_set, coercion dwiFiber S4 class 'dti' package. See Dell'Acqua, F., Descoteaux, M. Leemans, . (2024) "Handbook Diffusion MR Tractography" doi:10.1016/C2018-0-02520-7  mathematical computational underpinnings diffusion MRI tractography.

title: fiber: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography — fiber-package
text: Maintainer: Aymeric Stamm aymeric.stamm@cnrs.fr (ORCID) Authors: Aymeric Stamm aymeric.stamm@cnrs.fr (ORCID)

title: Curvature of a streamline — get_curvature
text: get_curvature() function computes curvature streamline object. curvature \(\kappa(s)\) point along arc-length abscissa computed using cubic smoothing splines (3 degrees freedom per component).

title: Curvature of a streamline — get_curvature
text: non-negative numeric vector length x@n_points giving curvature \(\kappa(s)\) sampled point along streamline. Higher values indicate sharper bending location.

title: Torsion of a streamline — get_torsion
text: numeric vector length x@n_points giving torsion \(\tau(s)\) sampled point along streamline. Positive values indicate right-handed twisting; negative values indicate left-handed twisting; zero indicates planar curve location.

what: reparametrize() method for bundle objects — reparametrize-fiber-bundle-method
title: reparametrize() method for bundle objects — reparametrize-fiber-bundle-method
text: Resamples every streamline inside bundle onto common uniform arc-length grid. See reparametrize() full parameter documentation.

title: reparametrize() method for bundle objects — reparametrize-fiber-bundle-method
text: x bundle object. n_points Number equally-spaced arc-length points use. single streamline, defaults x@n_points. bundle, defaults rounded mean number points across streamlines. Pass NULL use defaults explicitly.

title: reparametrize() method for bundle objects — reparametrize-fiber-bundle-method
text: bundle reparametrized onto new grid. Every streamline returned bundle exactly n_points rows @points (defaulting rounded mean number points across streamlines n_points NULL). @streamline_data @bundle_data preserved unchanged.

title: reparametrize() method for bundle objects — reparametrize-fiber-bundle-method
code: sl1 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl1, sl2)) b_reparam <- reparametrize(b, n_points = 8) b_reparam[[1]]@n_points  # 8 #> [1] 8 b_reparam[[2]]@n_points  # 8 #> [1] 8

what: reparametrize() method for streamline objects — reparametrize-fiber-streamline-method
title: reparametrize() method for streamline objects — reparametrize-fiber-streamline-method
text: Resamples single streamline onto uniform arc-length grid. See reparametrize() full parameter documentation.

title: reparametrize() method for streamline objects — reparametrize-fiber-streamline-method
text: x streamline object. n_points Number equally-spaced arc-length points use. single streamline, defaults x@n_points. bundle, defaults rounded mean number points across streamlines. Pass NULL use defaults explicitly.

title: reparametrize() method for streamline objects — reparametrize-fiber-streamline-method
text: streamline reparametrized onto new grid. returned object @points resampled exactly n_points rows via linear interpolation, numeric @point_data entries likewise resampled. Non-numeric @point_data entries dropped warning. @streamline_data preserved unchanged.

title: reparametrize() method for streamline objects — reparametrize-fiber-streamline-method
code: sl <- streamline(   points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10)),   point_data = list(FA = runif(10)) ) sl_reparam <- reparametrize(sl, n_points = 20) sl_reparam@n_points  # 20 #> [1] 20

what: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
text: reparametrize() S7 generic resamples 3-D coordinates (numeric @point_data attributes) tractography object onto uniform arc-length grid using linear interpolation, methods available following classes: fiber::bundle fiber::streamline

title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
code: reparametrize(x, n_points = NULL)

title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
text: x streamline bundle object. n_points Number equally-spaced arc-length points use. single streamline, defaults x@n_points. bundle, defaults rounded mean number points across streamlines. Pass NULL use defaults explicitly.

title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
text: object class x reparametrized onto new grid.

title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
code: # reparametrize a single streamline to 10 points sl <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) sl_reparam <- reparametrize(sl, n_points = 10) # reparametrize a bundle to the mean number of points across its streamlines sl1 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(5), Y = runif(5), Z = runif(5))) sl2 <- streamline(points = cbind(X = runif(10), Y = runif(10), Z = runif(10))) b <- bundle(streamlines = list(sl1, sl2)) bundle_reparam <- reparametrize(b)

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
text: streamline represents single fibre tract. stores three data compartments: @points — \(P \times 3\) numeric matrix column names "X", "Y", "Z" holding 3-D coordinates \(P\) points along tract. @point_data — named list numeric vectors, length \(P\), holding additional per-point scalar attributes (e.g. fractional anisotropy). Coordinates stored . @streamline_data — named list scalars (length-1 values type) holding per-streamline attributes (e.g. tract-level weight mean FA, character label).

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
code: streamline(points = NULL, point_data = list(), streamline_data = list())

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
text: points \(P \times 3\) numeric matrix column names "X", "Y", "Z" giving 3-D coordinates streamline points. point_data named list numeric vectors, length \(P\), holding additional per-point attributes. Must include "X", "Y", "Z" (live @points). streamline_data named list per-streamline scalars (length-1, type).

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
text: streamline S7 object.

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
text: following methods defined streamline objects: format(x, ...): Returns cli-formatted string describing streamline object. print(x, ...): Prints formatted string console invisibly returns x.

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
text: @n_points integer scalar giving number points streamline (read-). @point_attributes character vector names per-point attributes stored @point_data (read-). @streamline_attributes character vector names per-streamline attributes (read-).

title: Streamline S7 class — streamline
code:
# Create a streamline with 5 points and some attributes sl <- streamline(   points = cbind(     X = c(0, 1, 1, 1, 0),     Y = c(0, 0, 1, 1, 1),     Z = c(0, 0, 0, 1, 1)   ),   point_data = list(FA = c(0.5, 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9)),   streamline_data = list(mean_FA = 0.7, label = "CST") ) sl@n_points         # 5 #> [1] 5 sl@point_attributes # "FA" #> [1] "FA" sl@streamline_attributes # c("mean_FA", "label") #> [1] "mean_FA" "label"    # format() and print() methods format(sl) #> [1] "
── \033[1m\033[1mObject of class \033[1m`fiber::streamline()`\033[1m with \033[1m5\033[1m points.\033[1m\033[22m ──

\033[36m•\033[39m Point attributes: \033[34m"FA"\033[39m
\033[36m•\033[39m Streamline attributes: \033[34m"mean_FA"\033[39m and \033[34m"label"\033[39m
" print(sl) #>  #> ── Object of class `fiber::streamline()` with 5 points. ── #>  #> • Point attributes: "FA" #> • Streamline attributes: "mean_FA" and "label" #>

title: fiber 0.2.0
text: streamline: @point_data holds numeric-attributes (e.g. fractional anisotropy). bundle: @bundle_data now restricted named lists scalars (length-1 values, type). Previously arbitrary R objects accepted. Non-scalar values (e.g. affine matrices stored single @bundle_data entry) must split individual scalar entries moved elsewhere. bundle_set: @set_data now restricted named lists scalars (length-1 values, type), consistent @bundle_data bundle level.

title: fiber 0.2.0
text: format() print() methods streamline, bundle, bundle_set now use {cli} styled, ANSI-aware console output. Unknown shape descriptors passed add_shape_descriptors() now emit structured cli::cli_warn() messages instead base-R warnings.

title: fiber 0.2.0
text: bundle gains @streamline_data slot — named list vectors length SS (one entry per streamline) — per-streamline attributes aggregated bundle level. add_shape_descriptors() now stores scalar descriptors (euclidean_length, curvilinear_length, sinuosity) directly bundle@streamline_data rather individual streamline. bundle_set gains @bundle_data slot — named list vectors length BB (one entry per bundle) — per-bundle attributes aggregated set level. Automatic attribute lifting: constructing bundle, @streamline_data key present supplied streamlines automatically copied bundle@streamline_data length-SS vector. happens one level : common @bundle_data keys lifted bundle_set@bundle_data constructing bundle_set. Individual child slots dropped; parent-level values take precedence conflict. Subsetting push-: bundle[[]] now pushes corresponding bundle@streamline_data values back extracted streamlines’ @streamline_data. Likewise bundle_set[[]] pushes bundle_set@bundle_data values extracted bundles’ @bundle_data. bundle_set: @bundles longer needs named list. Names used populate bundle attribute vector called id_from_input_list names dropped input list. bundle() constructor gains streamline_data argument supplying per-streamline data explicitly (overrides automatic lifting). bind_bundles() gains streamline_data argument. bind_bundle_sets() gains bundle_data argument. Bare bundle arguments longer need named. as_bundle_set() method bundle: name argument now optional (default NULL); omitting creates unnamed single-element bundle_set.

title: fiber 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-30 Added bundle_set S7 class — named collection bundle objects multi-subject multi-session studies. Added is_bundle_set() predicate, format()/print()/length()/names()/[[/[ methods bundle_set. Added as_bundle_set() generic methods bundle_set (identity) bundle (wrap). Added bind_bundle_sets() combine named bundle objects /bundle_set objects single bundle_set. Relaxed streamline validator: @point_data entries longer need numeric (vector correct length accepted); @streamline_data entries longer need numeric either (scalar accepted). Non-numeric @point_data entries dropped warning reparametrize() called, since natural arc-length interpolant. Added missing \value documentation compute_hausdorff_distance() catch-method (#CRAN). Replaced \dontrun{} (requireNamespace("dti", quietly = TRUE)) {} as_dwifiber() example (#CRAN).

title: fiber 0.1.1
text: Improved documentation S7 classes, generics methods following release roxygen2 8.0.0. Removed redundant new_streamline() new_bundle() constructors. Added coercers dwiFiber S4 class dti package. Refactored shape descriptors API. Rewrote pairwise Hausdorff distance matrix using C++ via cpp11 package. Created tractoverse GitHub organization transferred fiber repository .

title: fiber 0.1.0
text: package rebuilt scratch around two S7 classes: streamline bundle. previous tibble-based streamline list-based tract removed.

title: fiber 0.1.0
text: new_streamline() / new_bundle() constructors S7 classes. bind_bundles() combines mix streamlines bundles single bundle. reparametrize() resamples streamline every streamline bundle onto uniform arc-length grid. get_euclidean_length(), get_curvilinear_length(), get_sinuosity() — geometric shape scalars. get_curvature(), get_torsion() — full curvature/torsion profiles summary scalars ("mean", "sd", "max"). get_hausdorff_distance() — symmetric Hausdorff distance two streamlines.

title: fiber 0.1.0
text: Runtime dependencies reduced S7, rlang, cli .
